contract_traceability: research/.done: only clarification questions produced, no upstream research contract available
architect/architecture_design.md: comprehensive design doc with traceability table (Step 5) linking research claims to architecture decisions
coder/: full implementation of all three lasso modes

baseline_status.baseline: loss_only mode (standard CNN + L1 loss penalty)
baseline_status.reason: loss_only config exists and is tested, but no training experiment compares proximal vs loss_only accuracy
External baseline not included in repository

grounded: L1 weight decay produces sparser weight matrices (well-known property of L1 regularization)
Group-lasso on filters induces structured sparsity (well-known in pruning literature)
Soft-thresholding with θ=0 reduces to identity
hypothesis: Architectural soft-thresholding produces sparser feature maps than loss-only L1 at equal accuracy
Per-channel learnable thresholds improve the accuracy-sparsity tradeoff vs. a fixed global threshold
LISTA unrolling provides theoretical convergence guarantees for the forward pass
TODO_unverified: Proximal mode outperforms loss-only mode on accuracy-sparsity Pareto frontier
Group-lasso achieves >30% filter sparsity without >1% accuracy drop
Adaptive threshold successfully prevents neuron death during training
Tied-weights LISTA matches untied-weights accuracy at fewer parameters

evidence: architect/architecture_design.md Step 3: clearly articulates replacing ReLU with soft-thresholding as architectural lasso integration
coder/layers.py: implements SoftThreshold, AdaptiveSoftThreshold, HardThreshold as nn.Module classes
coder/blocks.py LassoProxConvBlock: soft-thresholding integrated into forward pass, not just loss function
concerns: No literature survey or related-work analysis (research stage incomplete)
Adaptive soft-threshold is well-known (Gregor & LeCun 2010) — incremental contribution
No experimental evidence that the approach differs meaningfully from vanilla L1 regularization on loss

evidence: validator/test_model.py: 30+ pytest tests covering shapes, gradients, numerics, sparsity, loss, checkpointing
validator/ablate.py: all 6 architect-proposed ablations (A1-A6) implemented as single-field config changes
validator/profile.py: torch.profiler script for forward, train, and memory profiling
test_model.py: CV domain tests (translation robustness, noise entropy, sparsity profile)
concerns: BLOCKING: No training loop implemented — cannot measure accuracy or validate the core claim
BLOCKING: No baseline comparison (external: ResNet-18; internal: proximal vs loss_only)
No experiment tracking, no seed control, no hyperparameter search
No data pipeline (CIFAR-10 loading, augmentation)

evidence: architect/architecture_design.md Step 3 (gradient flow analysis) and Step 8 (loss formulation)
coder/loss.py: composite loss function well-documented with three penalty terms
coder/blocks.py ListaEncoder: rigorous connection to ISTA optimization documented
architect/architecture_design.md Step 7: four implementation risks identified with mitigations
concerns: No stationary-point analysis of the proximal-mode training objective (non-smooth due to soft-threshold)
Adaptive soft-threshold is not the proximal operator of any convex regularizer — docs acknowledge this but do not analyze the gap
No convergence rate analysis for LISTA unrolling (standard rate O(1/k) for ISTA; does unrolling preserve this?)

evidence: coder/smoke_test.py: basic shape + gradient verification at initialization
validator/ablate.py dry-run mode: measures parameter count and sparsity at init for all ablations
concerns: CRITICAL: No training results exist for any experiment
All measurements are at initialization (random weights) — not informative for validation
No accuracy, no loss curves, no sparsity-vs-accuracy tradeoff measurements
No error analysis or failure mode characterization

evidence: coder/: Complete, well-documented implementation with clear module boundaries
config.py: single dataclass for all hyperparameters with sensible defaults
test_model.py: comprehensive pytest suite (30+ tests)
layers.py: bf16/fp16 safety in all proximal operators
blocks.py LassoProxConvBlock: gradient checkpointing support
concerns: No setup.py or requirements.txt (depends on sys.path.insert for imports)
No training script — model can be instantiated and profiled but not trained end-to-end
Smoke test imports via sys.path.insert — fragile for external users

evidence: architect/architecture_design.md: high-quality write-up with diagrams, traceability, and risk analysis
All code has extensive docstrings with mathematical notation
concerns: No experimental results to report in any publication
No related-work section (upstream research stage incomplete)
No ablation results table (no training experiments)
No figures, no tables of numerical results

blocking_gaps.description: No training loop exists — the core claim (proximal vs loss_only accuracy-sparsity tradeoff) cannot be evaluated
No baseline comparison implemented internally (loss_only) or externally (ResNet-18)
6 claims marked as 'hypothesis' or 'TODO_unverified' require experimental results to be grounded
No training data pipeline, no experiment tracking, no hyperparameter tuning
A5 (LISTA tied vs untied) can only be run in dry-run mode; no accuracy comparison available
No literature survey comparing against L1-regularized CNNs, pruning methods, or LISTA variants

recommended_next_experiments.experiment: Train proximal vs loss_only mode on CIFAR-10
recommended_next_experiments.description: Train both modes with identical architecture (stage_channels=(64,128,256,512), stage_depths=(2,2,2,2)) for 200 epochs. Report test accuracy and activation sparsity at convergence.
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: Proximal mode should match loss-only accuracy within 1% while achieving >20% activation sparsity
recommended_next_experiments.falsification: If accuracy drops >3% or sparsity <5%, the architectural integration provides no benefit over loss-only L1

recommended_next_experiments.description: Train each ablation config for 100 epochs, measure accuracy and sparsity. Rank by accuracy-sparsity Pareto efficiency.
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: A1 (loss_only) lowest sparsity; A4 (adaptive) most stable training; A3 (no group lasso) highest accuracy
recommended_next_experiments.falsification: If A1 matches A0 on sparsity, architectural prox is unnecessary

recommended_next_experiments.description: Train ResNet-18 with standard L2 decay. Compare accuracy, parameter count, and inference speed against LassoConvNet.
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: LassoConvNet should match ResNet-18 accuracy with sparser activations
recommended_next_experiments.falsification: If ResNet-18 achieves higher accuracy at same parameter count, the architecture underperforms standard designs

recommended_next_experiments.description: Track per-channel theta values during training. Measure: (a) do thresholds converge? (b) is there channel specialization? (c) does theta oscillate in late training?
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: Thresholds should converge to distinct per-channel values, with higher thresholds on less informative channels

recommended_next_experiments.description: Compare soft vs adaptive threshold during training. Measure: dead neuron fraction, gradient norm, and final accuracy.
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: Adaptive threshold should have fewer dead channels (near-zero gradient from dead zone)

recommended_next_experiments.description: Remove zero-filters (from group lasso), export without threshold ops (set θ→0). Measure compressed model accuracy and inference speedup.
Compare tied vs untied LISTA on CIFAR-10. Measure: accuracy vs number of iterations, reconstruction error (MSE of D*Z vs X).
recommended_next_experiments.expected_outcome: Tied weights should match untied at 50-70% of the parameters
